name: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution

sourceRefs.label: tnpscguru.in - dedicated TNPSC Indian Polity MCQ set (Part XI, 11th Schedule, Article 356/370, 8th Schedule, CAG, Judicial Review)
drishtiias.com / vajiramandravi.com - federal-unitary blend, length, K.C. Wheare's 'quasi-federal' description
standard civics facts (Articles/Parts/Schedules counts, DPSP's Irish origin) — well-established

researchNotes: OCR index search for 'salient feature/federal unitary/written constitution/parliamentary system' returned zero hits across all 730 question-bank pages — web research only, second topic in a row with no local past-paper evidence (consistent with the broader Unit IV pattern so far). The tnpscguru.in set is real exam-grade and clusters heavily on SCHEDULES and landmark CASES rather than plain definitions — a strong signal that TNPSC tests the Constitution's mechanics (which schedule covers what, which case decided what) over textbook-style description. Article 370 is included as a real exam fact about the Constitution's ORIGINAL text and intent; flagging clearly that it was abrogated by a Presidential Order in August 2019 — a past-paper question asking 'what does Article 370 contain' is testing the historical/original provision, not current status, so both the original content and the 2019 change are noted to avoid an outdated-fact trap.

reading.intro: Covers the Constitution's defining structural characteristics — its length, its federal-unitary blend, and a cluster of real exam facts about specific Schedules, Articles, and landmark Supreme Court cases. Distinct from the Preamble topic (a4-constitution-preamble-overview), which covers the Preamble's text and amendment history specifically.

qa.question: What makes the Indian Constitution notable for its length, and how has this changed since 1949?
qa.answer: It is the longest written constitution of any sovereign country in the world. Originally (1949) it had 395 Articles, 22 Parts, and 8 Schedules; through amendments it has grown to roughly 448 Articles, 25 Parts, and 12 Schedules today (exact counts shift slightly as articles are added/repealed).

qa.question: Is India's Constitution federal or unitary in character?
qa.answer: A blend of both — often called 'quasi-federal' (a term associated with constitutional scholar K.C. Wheare): federal in FORM (a written, supreme constitution; division of powers between Centre and States; bicameral Parliament; independent judiciary) but unitary in SPIRIT, with a single integrated judiciary, single citizenship, a flexible amendment process for many provisions, Centrally-appointed Governors, All India Services (IAS/IPS/IFS), and strong emergency powers that can centralise power temporarily.

qa.question: What form of government does the Constitution establish at the Centre?
qa.answer: A parliamentary form of government: the President is the nominal/constitutional head of state, while real executive authority rests with the Prime Minister and Council of Ministers, who are collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha (the directly elected house).

qa.question: Where did India borrow its Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP) and Judicial Review from?
qa.answer: The Directive Principles of State Policy were adopted from the Irish Constitution. Judicial Review (the power of courts to strike down unconstitutional laws) is an important contribution borrowed from the Constitution of the USA (per a real TNPSC question).

qa.question: Real exam: What does Part XI of the Constitution describe?
qa.answer: Centre-State relations (legislative, administrative, and financial relations between the Union and the States) — not to be confused with the Union Government (covered in Part V) or State Government (Part VI).

qa.question: Real exam: Which Schedule lists the subjects assigned to Panchayats, and what is NOT included in it?
qa.answer: The Eleventh Schedule specifies the powers, authority, and responsibilities of Panchayats, listing 29 subjects (agriculture, minor irrigation, social forestry, rural housing, drinking water, health and sanitation, public distribution system, etc.). 'Prevention of cruelty to animals' is NOT one of the 29 items — a real exam question tests this exclusion specifically, so don't assume every plausible-sounding welfare/animal-related subject is on the list.

qa.question: Real exam: Which Schedule of the Constitution deals with languages, and what was added to it by the 21st Amendment Act, 1967?
qa.answer: The Eighth Schedule lists the officially recognised languages of India. The 21st Constitutional Amendment Act, 1967 added Sindhi to this Schedule.

qa.question: Real exam: To whom is the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India responsible, and who chaired the Official Language Commission of 1955?
qa.answer: The CAG is responsible only to Parliament (not to the President or Prime Minister individually) — their audit reports are submitted to the President/Governors but are placed before Parliament and State Legislatures. B.G. Kher chaired the Official Language Commission, 1955 (a real TNPSC exam fact).

qa.question: Real exam: Which landmark Supreme Court case dealt with the misuse of Article 356 (President's Rule), and what does Article 370 (in its original form) contain?
qa.answer: The S.R. Bommai case (1994) is the landmark judgement curbing arbitrary use of Article 356 to dismiss State governments. Article 370, in the Constitution's original text, granted special autonomous status to Jammu & Kashmir — note: this provision was effectively abrogated by a Presidential Order in August 2019, so treat 'what did Article 370 contain' as a question about the original/historical provision.

questions.prompt: The Constitution of India is notable for being:
options: The shortest written constitution in the world
An entirely unwritten constitution like the UK's
The longest written constitution of any sovereign country
A purely federal constitution with no unitary features
questions.explanation: Longest written constitution in the world.

questions.prompt: The Indian Constitution is best described, per constitutional scholar K.C. Wheare, as:
options: Quasi-federal — federal in form, unitary in spirit

questions.prompt: Part XI of the Indian Constitution describes:
Which one of the following is NOT an item in the Eleventh Schedule of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Prevention of cruelty to animals is not among the 29 Panchayat subjects in the Eleventh Schedule.

questions.prompt: Which Schedule of the Constitution specifies the powers, authority, and responsibility of Panchayats?
Which language was added to the Eighth Schedule by the 21st Constitutional Amendment Act, 1967?
Which Schedule of the Indian Constitution deals with the officially recognised languages?
The Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India is responsible only to:
Who chaired the Official Language Commission of 1955?
Judicial Review, as a feature of the Indian Constitution, is an important contribution borrowed from:
The Supreme Court's landmark judgement curbing misuse of Article 356 (President's Rule) was delivered in:
In its original (pre-2019) form, Article 370 of the Constitution dealt with:
questions.explanation: Autonomous status of J&K — note this provision was abrogated by Presidential Order in August 2019.

questions.prompt: The Directive Principles of State Policy in the Indian Constitution were adopted from the Constitution of:
